hero.label: Secure Data Transfer
hero.title:
Move data <1>directly</1>.
No cloud.
No middleman.
hero.description: Mirall connects your devices directly. Create a space, invite collaborators, and move files that travel straight between devices — encrypted end-to-end, and completely serverless.
hero.betaNotice: Mirall is currently in beta. Some features may change and you might encounter bugs.
hero.industries: Trusted for post-production, architecture, legal, and research workflows.

features.title: Built different
features.description: No accounts. No uploads to someone else's server. Mirall gives you a private space to share files the way it should work.

spaces.title: Shared Spaces
spaces.description: Create topic-based spaces for your team, project, or group. Everyone in a space sees all available files and chooses what to download.

direct.title: Direct Device-to-Device
direct.description: Files move straight between your devices over encrypted connections. No server ever sees your data.

encrypted.title: Encrypted by Default
encrypted.description: Every connection between devices is end-to-end encrypted. No keys to manage — it just works.

inviteOnly.title: Invite-Only Access
inviteOnly.description: Share a simple invite code to add people to a space. No accounts, no sign-ups, no permission dashboards.

explicitDownload.title: You Choose What to Keep
explicitDownload.description: See every file available in a space. Download only what you need — nothing syncs without your say-so.

zeroInfra.title: Zero Infrastructure
zeroInfra.description: No cloud storage fees. No server maintenance. Mirall is fully decentralized — files move directly between your devices.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.description: Three steps. No setup wizards, no admin panels.

items.title: Create a space
items.description: Name it, pick an icon, and you're live. Mirall generates a unique invite code you can share with anyone.

items.title: Invite your people
items.description: Send the invite code. Your collaborators paste it in and they're connected — directly to you, no server in between.

items.title: Share and download
items.description: Drop files into the space. Everyone sees what's available. Each person downloads exactly what they need.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.description: Everything you need to know about Mirall.

items.question: Where are my files stored?
items.answer: On your device, and only your device. When you share a file, other members download it directly from you. There is no cloud server, no intermediary storage. Once someone downloads a file, they have their own local copy.

items.question: What happens when I'm offline?
items.answer: Files you've downloaded stay on your device — they're yours. Other members can still see the file metadata, but they can't download files from you until you're back online. If another member who has the same file is online, they can download from them instead.

items.question: Is it really encrypted?
items.answer: Yes. Every connection between devices is end-to-end encrypted. No one — not even us — can see your files or metadata in transit. There's no central server that could be breached.

items.question: How do I invite someone to a space?
items.answer: When you create a space, Mirall generates an invite code. Share it however you like — chat, email, text. They paste it into the app and they're connected instantly.

items.question: What languages does Mirall support?
items.answer: English, German, French, Spanish, and Italian. Mirall picks up your OS language on first launch; you can change it any time in Settings → Language.

items.question: Are there file size limits?
items.answer: No hard limits. Mirall streams files in chunks, so large files work fine. Transfer speed depends on your network connection and the sender's upload bandwidth.

items.question: Is Mirall free?
items.answer: Mirall is free to use for now, but we plan to charge for it in the future.

items.question: How do I keep Mirall up to date?
items.answer: You don't have to. Once installed, Mirall updates itself in the background and the new version is live the next time you launch — no reinstalls, no installer downloads.

items.question: How is Mirall different from Aspera, MASV, or WeTransfer?
items.answer: Those services all route or store your files on their own infrastructure, which means per-GB fees, transfer ceilings, and a cloud vendor in the middle of your data. Mirall transfers files directly between devices with no servers in between — so there are no per-GB costs, no transfer caps, and nothing to breach.

items.question: Is Mirall suitable for GDPR, Schrems II, or HIPAA workflows?
items.answer: Yes. Because Mirall never stores your files on a third-party server, there is no cross-border data transfer or cloud processor relationship to worry about — the jurisdictional question doesn't apply. Many regulated teams in healthcare, legal, and post-production use this as an architectural answer to GDPR, Schrems II, and HIPAA concerns.

download.label: Download
download.title: Download Mirall
download.description: Available for macOS, Windows, and Linux. Pick your platform and start moving data directly between devices in seconds. Once installed, Mirall keeps itself up to date — new versions arrive in the background.
download.betaNotice: This is a beta release — we are looking for your feedback.

preview.label: Preview
preview.title: Mirall preview build
preview.description: Internal staging channel for testing the next Mirall release before it ships to production. Builds here are signed and installable, but may contain unfinished features or bugs.
preview.warning: Preview builds are pre-release and may be unstable. Don't install on a machine you depend on.

cta.title: Your files. Your devices. No middleman.
cta.description: Join the movement that share files the way it should be — direct, encrypted, and free from cloud lock-in.

footer.tagline: Secure data transfer for teams. No cloud, no accounts, no compromises.

support.title: Support
support.heading: Get help from inside the app
support.description: Mirall has built-in support. Click the "Send Feedback" button in the top right corner of the app to report issues, request features, or ask questions. Add your email if you'd like a reply, and Mirall attaches version and platform diagnostics so we can debug faster. Your message goes directly to our team.
support.screenshotAlt: Mirall app with the Send Feedback button highlighted

steps.title: How to reach us
items: Open Mirall on your desktop
Click the "Send Feedback" button in the top right corner
Describe your issue or question — add your email if you want a reply
Hit send — we'll get back to you

support.note: No external ticketing system, no email chains. Everything stays in the app.

hub.label: Documentation
hub.heading: Mirall documentation
hub.intro: Mirall is a secure data transfer app for desktop. No cloud, no accounts — files move directly between you and the people you invite. The docs are organized into four kinds of material, so you can find what fits the moment you're in.

cards.title: Tutorials
cards.description: New to Mirall? Start here. Guided, end-to-end lessons that get you to a first success — sending files and sharing a folder.

cards.title: How-to guides
cards.description: Know what you want to do? Step-by-step recipes for a single task — create a space, mirror a folder, reclaim disk space, and more.

cards.title: Reference
cards.description: Need the facts? Complete tables of file statuses, folder types, keyboard shortcuts, settings, and the application menu.

cards.title: Explanation
cards.description: Want to understand the why? How peer-to-peer works, the privacy model, mirroring, on-demand sharing, and how storage reclaim works.

tutorials.label: Tutorials
tutorials.heading: Tutorials
tutorials.intro: Guided lessons for newcomers. Follow them top to bottom — each one takes you all the way through a real task so you learn Mirall by doing.

items.title: Send your first files
items.intro: In a few minutes you'll install Mirall, create a space, invite someone, and watch a file travel directly from your device to theirs. You only need a second person with Mirall installed to receive — or a second computer of your own.

items.title: Install and open Mirall
items.text: Download Mirall for your platform from the download page and open it. The first time it launches, Mirall asks for a **display name** and an optional **avatar** — that's how other members will see you. Your OS language is detected automatically.

items.title: Create a space
items.text: On the **Shared Spaces** screen, click **Create Space**, give it a name, pick an icon, and click **Initialize Space**. A space is a private group where you and the people you invite share files.

items.title: Copy the invite
items.text: Mirall shows an invite right away. Copy it and send it to the person you want to share with — over chat, email, anything. The invite carries the space name, so they'll see what they're joining.

items.title: Let them join
items.text: The other person clicks **Join Space**, pastes the invite, and confirms. Once you're both online, you'll see each other in the **Members** panel.

items.title: Share a file
items.text: Open your space and drag a file onto the **Drop to Share** zone, or click **Select Files**. Mirall prepares it and makes it available to everyone in the space.

items.title: Download on the other side
items.text: On the other person's Mirall, the file appears with a download button. They click it and the file transfers directly from your device to theirs — with live progress, speed, and ETA. That's it: no server ever touched the file.

blocks.alt: Mirall onboarding screen — 'Welcome to Mirall' card prompting for a display name and avatar
Mirall space view — files shared by you, the Drop to Share zone, and the Members panel

blocks.text: Nothing downloads automatically. Members always see what's available and choose what to pull onto their own device.

items.title: Share a folder and keep it in sync
items.intro: New in 1.6: instead of picking files one by one, you can share a whole folder into a space and keep it in sync. This lesson walks you through sharing a folder, then having another member mirror it to their own disk.

items.title: Open a space
items.text: Open an existing space, or create one as in the first tutorial. Anything you share lives inside a space.

items.title: Add a folder
items.text: Open **Add Folder** (from the share area in the sidebar, the **File** menu, or the keyboard shortcut). Pick a folder on your computer and give it a **share name** — that's how the folder appears to other members. Subfolders are included automatically.

items.title: Choose how it's shared
items.text: Pick **Eager** to pre-prepare a full copy so peers can download instantly, or **On demand** to add the folder instantly and prepare each file only when someone requests it. On demand is best for large folders or limited disk space.

items.title: Confirm
items.text: Click **Add Folder**. Mirall scans the folder and starts offering its files to the space. From now on, when you add, edit, or remove files in that folder on your computer, the change syncs to the space on its own.

items.title: Have a member mirror it
items.text: Another member opens the shared folder. They can **browse** it — open it and download individual files — or click **Mirror to Disk** to keep a live, always-up-to-date copy in a folder of their choosing.

items.title: Watch it stay in sync
items.text: Change a file in your shared folder. On the mirroring member's computer, the mirror updates to match. The mirror is **read-only**: if they edit a mirrored file, it's restored to your version on the next sync.

blocks.alt: A Mirall space with a shared folder — the Production Footage folder card marked 'Shared by you', with File Storage and Members panels in the sidebar

blocks.text: Not sure which sharing mode to pick? See **Eager vs on-demand sharing** in Explanation for the trade-off.

guides.label: How-to guides
guides.heading: How-to guides
guides.intro: Task-focused recipes. Each one assumes you already know the basics and gets you straight to a single goal.

items.text: Click **Create Space** on the Shared Spaces screen.
Give the space a name and pick an icon.
Click **Initialize Space** — Mirall generates an invite and shows it right away.
Copy the invite and send it to anyone you want to add.
Click **Join Space** on the Shared Spaces screen.
Paste the invite you received.
Optionally give the space a local name — only you will see it.
Click **Join Space**. Once another member is online, their files appear automatically.

blocks.text: Click **Invite** at the top of a space to open the invite dialog, then pick a format:

items: **Code** drops a short text code into your clipboard.
**App link** gives you a clickable `mirall://` URL that opens Mirall straight to the Join dialog (the recipient needs Mirall installed).

blocks.text: Send it however you like — chat, email, anything works. Invites carry the space name, so the person joining sees what they're joining instead of a generic "Shared Space". The same invite can be shared with as many people as you like. Click **Done** when you're finished.

image.alt: Mirall 'Invite to Space' dialog with a Code / App link toggle set to App link, a copyable mirall://join/… URL, and a Done button

blocks.text: Star any space to pin it to **Favorites**. On the Shared Spaces screen you can filter the list by **All Spaces** or **Favorites**. You can also add a space to Favorites from the **More** menu inside the space.

Open the space, open the **More** menu, and choose **Leave Space**. Mirall cancels any in-flight transfers and removes the space's cached data from your device. Files you've already saved to your Downloads folder are untouched — they stay yours.

Drag files onto the **Drop to Share** zone in the sidebar, or click **Select Files** to pick them from your computer. Mirall adds them to the space and starts making them available to other members right away.

Very large files (up to 1 TB) work fine — content is streamed in chunks rather than uploaded as a whole. Mirall still needs a moment to chunk the file before sharing can start, so expect a short preparation step for very large files.
Want to share a whole folder instead of picking files? See **Share a whole folder**.

Click the download button on any file shared by another member. The file transfers directly from their device to yours with live progress, speed, and ETA. Downloads land in your chosen download folder.

docs.title: Pause, resume, retry, or discard a transfer

blocks.text: If your network drops or the member you're downloading from goes offline, the transfer pauses automatically and resumes from where it left off once a member with that file is back. You can also manage transfers by hand:

items: **Pause** — stop an in-progress download; its progress is kept so you can resume later.
**Resume** — continue an interrupted download from where it stopped.
**Retry** — reattempt a download that ended in an error.
**Discard partial** — throw away a paused or failed download and free the space it was using.

blocks.text: In-progress downloads are written to a hidden `.partial` file and renamed to the final name only when the transfer completes — an interrupted transfer can't leave a half-written file under its real name.

Use a file's menu and choose **Unshare** to remove a file you added so it's no longer offered to other members. Files they've already downloaded stay on their devices — unsharing only stops offering new copies.

docs.title: Share a whole folder
docs.intro: New in 1.6. Share a folder into a space and Mirall keeps it in sync — add, edit, or remove files and everyone in the space sees the change automatically. Subfolders come along too.

items.text: Open the space you want to share into.
Open **Add Folder** from the share area in the sidebar, the **File** menu, or the keyboard shortcut.
Choose a folder on your computer and give it a **share name** — how it appears to other members. The name must be unique within the space.
Pick a sharing mode: **Eager** (pre-prepare a full copy for instant downloads) or **On demand** (add instantly, prepare each file when requested).
Click **Add Folder**. Mirall scans the folder and starts offering its files. Changes you make to the folder later sync to the space automatically.

blocks.text: Folders you share are marked **Shared by you**. Not sure which mode to pick? See **Eager vs on-demand sharing**.

image.alt: Mirall's Add Folder dialog — folder path, a share name, and a Folder Share toggle set to Eager with On demand as the alternative

blocks.text: Open a folder another member shared to see what's inside without downloading everything. Click **Download** on any individual file to save just that one, or choose **Mirror to Disk** to keep the whole folder synced. A folder you're only looking through is marked **Browse**.

image.alt: Browsing a shared folder in Mirall — six files each marked Available with a download button, and a sidebar explaining browse versus mirror

docs.title: Mirror a folder to disk
docs.intro: New in 1.6. Keep a live, always-up-to-date copy of a shared folder in a folder on your own computer.

items.text: Open the shared folder and click **Mirror to Disk**.
Choose a location on your computer for the mirror.
Confirm to start mirroring. Mirall downloads the contents and keeps them in sync as the owner makes changes.

blocks.text: A mirror is **read-only**. If you edit a file inside the mirror, Mirall restores it to the owner's version on the next sync. To keep your own edits, copy the file out of the mirror folder first.

image.alt: Mirall's Mirror to Disk dialog — pick a location for a read-only copy, with a note that local edits revert to the owner's version on the next sync

docs.title: Pause, resume, move, or stop a mirror

items: **Pause Mirror** — stop syncing for now; the folder stays where it is and won't update until you resume.
**Resume Mirror** — start syncing again from wherever the owner's copy is now.
**Change Mirror Location** — move the mirror to a different folder on your computer.
**Unmount Mirror** — stop mirroring entirely and remove the mirrored copy.

image.alt: A mirrored folder in Mirall — every file marked 'On your device', a Mirrored read-only badge, and the read-only mirror explanation in the sidebar

blocks.text: Open **Settings → Storage**. The **Download folder** entry at the top lets you pick any folder via your OS picker. Every download from every space lands there from then on, and the choice persists across restarts. It defaults to your OS Downloads folder.

In **Settings → Storage**, each space you've joined has a row with a **More** menu. Use it to drop locally-cached content from peers in just that space, without leaving it. Your own published files stay, and you can re-download anything from peers any time they're online.
Files saved to your Downloads folder aren't part of a space's cache and aren't affected. Leaving a space clears its cache automatically.

Storage settings now show how much each shared folder is using, and how much can be **reclaimed**. Reclaimable space builds up when files in shared folders are edited or replaced — Mirall keeps old versions around briefly so transfers in flight can finish.

Mirall reclaims this space automatically once it grows large and the space goes quiet. To reclaim it yourself, open a space's actions in **Settings → Storage** and choose **Reclaim space**. Your current files are unaffected — only old, superseded versions are dropped.

image.alt: Mirall Storage settings — Total Storage and per-space usage, with the Production Footage shared folder showing 18.2 MB across 6 files

blocks.text: Click your avatar in the top-right to open the **Account** page. Under **Profile** you can change your display name and avatar — changes propagate to every space you're in. Your profile is only shared with the peers you connect with.

Your avatar gains a soft pulsing ring when something's wrong — red if you've lost internet, amber while Mirall is trying to recover. No ring means you're connected and ready. When a problem appears, a toast notification offers a **Show details** link.

Open **Account → Network** for a friendly diagnostics view — useful when a friend can't reach you or a transfer stalls. It explains what Mirall sees about your connection, points out things worth checking (router, VPN, firewall), and includes a **Reconnect** button to nudge things along.

Mirall lives in your menu bar (macOS) or system tray (Windows and Linux). Closing the window keeps the app running so peers stay connected, transfers keep going, and notifications still arrive — click the tray icon to bring the window back.
**Settings → General** lets you opt out of background mode, or turn on **Launch at login** so Mirall starts quietly with your computer.

Mirall ships in five languages — English, German, French, Spanish, and Italian. Your OS locale is detected on first launch. Switch any time under **Settings → Appearance → Language**; your choice persists across restarts.

docs.title: Change the theme and display size

blocks.text: Under **Settings → Appearance**, pick a **Theme Mode** — Light, Dark, or System (follows your OS automatically). Set the **Display size** to Compact, Cozy, Default, or Spacious, or step through any zoom level with `Cmd/Ctrl +`, `−`, and `0`. Your choice persists across restarts.

Mirall surfaces incoming files and space activity through your operating system's notification center. Open **Settings → Notifications** to fine-tune what shows up — a master switch, whether the system sound plays, and whether Mirall stays quiet while its window is in front.
Per-event toggles let you opt in or out of: someone comes online, someone goes offline, a download finishes, a download fails, a download pauses (sender went offline), and a download resumes.
You don't have to do anything — Mirall checks for new versions on its own and downloads them in the background. A staged update is applied the next time you quit and reopen Mirall, on your schedule.

When an update is waiting, a banner appears at the top of the window (you can dismiss it), and the pending version is also shown on the **Settings → About** screen so you can always check what's coming.

reference.label: Reference
reference.heading: Reference
reference.intro: Complete, lookup-able facts about Mirall — statuses, types, shortcuts, settings, and menus.

sections.title: File statuses
sections.intro: What each label on a file means.

rows: A file you added to the space. It lives on your device and is offered to other members.
A file you downloaded from another member. It's yours to keep and works offline.
Another member is sharing this file and at least one copy is online. Click download to pull it.
Mirall is finishing preparing a file you just added so other members can fetch it.
An on-demand file is being prepared by its owner before your download can start.
A transfer is in progress. The card shows bytes, speed, and ETA.
A partial download paused due to a network issue or because you paused it. Click play to resume.
Every member who has this file is currently offline. It will become available when one reconnects.
A transfer errored out. Click retry to try again, or dismiss it.
The owner removed this file from their drive while a download was in progress. Mirall frees the partial and tells you what happened.

sections.title: Folder & share types
sections.intro: New in 1.6. The badge on a shared folder tells you your relationship to it.

rows: A folder on your computer that you share into the space. Your local changes sync to the space automatically.
A folder another member shares that you're looking through. Download individual files, or mirror the whole folder.
A folder another member shares that you keep as a live, read-only copy on your disk. It stays in sync as the owner changes it.
A mirror whose syncing you've paused. Files won't update until you resume it.

sections.title: The space sidebar
sections.intro: New in 1.6. Each space view has a sidebar with a Drop to Share zone plus two panels — File Storage and Members — that you can collapse to fold away what you're not using.

image.alt: A Mirall space sidebar with the File Storage panel collapsed and the Members panel expanded, showing the collapsible panels

sections.title: File & folder actions
list: **Reveal in folder** — open a downloaded or shared file in your OS file browser.
**Unshare** — remove a file you added so it's no longer offered (files others already downloaded stay on their devices).
**Pause** / **Resume** — stop an in-progress download and continue it later from where it stopped.
**Discard partial** — throw away a paused or failed download and free the space.
**Retry** — reattempt a download that ended in an error.
**Mirror to Disk** — keep a live, read-only copy of a shared folder on your computer.
**Pause / Resume Mirror** — stop or restart a mirror's syncing.
**Change Mirror Location** — move a mirror to a different folder.
**Unmount Mirror** — stop mirroring and remove the mirrored copy.
**Reclaim space** — drop old, superseded versions of files in a space to free disk space.

sections.title: Keyboard shortcuts
sections.intro: Press the command palette shortcut to jump to any space or run any action; press the shortcuts shortcut for this list inside the app.

rows: Join a space with an invite
Add files to the current space
Go back to the previous screen

sections.title: Getting around
sections.intro: New in 1.6. Move through Mirall the way you would in a browser.
list: **Back** — your mouse's back button, a two-finger swipe on macOS, `Cmd/Ctrl + ←`, or **View → Back**.
**Home** — click the Mirall logo, or press `Ctrl-H` (`Cmd-Shift-H` on macOS) to return to your Shared Spaces.
**Command palette** — `Cmd/Ctrl + K` to search and jump to any space or run any action.

sections.title: Settings
sections.intro: The gear icon opens Settings — everything about the app itself. Your profile and network connection live separately on the Account page (click your avatar).

rows: Background mode (menu bar / system tray) and Launch at login.
Theme Mode (Light / Dark / System), Display size, and Language.
Master switch, system sound, mute-when-focused, and per-event toggles.
Download folder, total and per-space usage, reclaimable space, and per-space cache controls.
Installed version, any pending update, documentation and support links, and What's new.

sections.title: Application menu
sections.intro: New in 1.6. The application menu is now available on Windows and Linux as well as macOS.

rows: Add Files, Add Folder, and Invite to Space (available when a space is open); Quit.
What's New, Documentation, Send Feedback, and About Mirall.

sections.title: Platforms & languages
list: **Platforms** — macOS, Windows, and Linux (x64 and ARM64).
**Languages** — English, German, French, Spanish, and Italian, auto-detected from your OS on first launch.
**Updates** — delivered automatically in the background and applied when you next quit and reopen.
**File sizes** — no hard limit; very large files (up to 1 TB) stream in chunks.

explanation.label: Explanation
explanation.heading: Explanation
explanation.intro: Background and mental models — the why behind how Mirall behaves.

topics.title: How Mirall works: peer-to-peer, no server

blocks.text: Mirall connects your devices directly. When you share a file, its contents stay on your device; other members download it straight from you over an encrypted connection. There is no cloud server in the middle storing or relaying your files.

What does travel ahead of time is lightweight **metadata** — names, sizes, and which member is offering what — so everyone can see what's available in a space. The actual bytes of a file move only when someone chooses to download it.
Every connection between devices is end-to-end encrypted. No one — not even us — can see your files or metadata in transit, and there's no central server that could be breached.

There are no accounts and no tracking. Access to a space is **invite-only**: only people you give an invite to can see what you share. Your profile (display name and avatar) is shared only with the peers you connect with.

A space is a private group with its own members and its own files. Because files live on members' devices rather than a server, a file is only downloadable while at least one member who has it is **online**.

If everyone offering a file goes offline, the file is marked accordingly until someone returns. If several members have the same file, you can download it from whoever is online — so the more members keep a copy, the more available it stays.
New in 1.6. When you share a folder, you choose how Mirall prepares it.

**Eager** pre-imports a full second copy of the folder when you add it. Peers can download files immediately with no per-file delay, but it uses roughly twice the disk space, and the initial scan can take a while for large folders.

**On demand** adds the folder instantly with no second copy on disk. Each file is prepared just-in-time when a peer requests it, so first-time downloads carry a brief delay and you need to be online to serve them. It's the better choice for large folders or when disk space is limited.
New in 1.6. When you mirror a shared folder to disk, you get a live copy that tracks the owner's folder. The owner is the single source of truth, which is why a mirror is **read-only**.

If you change a file inside a mirror, the next sync restores it to the owner's version — your edit is discarded. This keeps every mirror faithful to the original. To work on a file, copy it out of the mirror folder first; to propose changes back, share them with the owner separately.

Mirall keeps two kinds of data on your device: files you've **shared** (the originals on your disk) and content you've **cached** from peers (files you downloaded or are mirroring). Storage settings break this down per space.

When files in a shared folder are edited or replaced, Mirall briefly keeps the old versions so transfers already in flight can finish. Over time these superseded versions become **reclaimable** space. Mirall reclaims it automatically once it grows large and the space goes quiet, and you can reclaim it yourself at any time from a space's actions in Storage settings. Reclaiming never touches your current files.
Mirall checks for new versions on its own and downloads them in the background while you keep working — no installer downloads, no reinstalls. Updates never restart the app on their own.
A staged update is applied the next time you quit and reopen Mirall, so restarts happen on your schedule. You can see a pending version on the Settings → About screen before you restart.

Services like Aspera, MASV, or WeTransfer route or store your files on their own infrastructure — which means per-GB fees, transfer ceilings, and a cloud vendor in the middle of your data. Mirall transfers files directly between devices with no servers in between, so there are no per-GB costs, no transfer caps, and nothing to breach.

Because Mirall never stores your files on a third-party server, there is no cross-border data transfer or cloud-processor relationship to reason about — the jurisdictional question doesn't arise. Many regulated teams in healthcare, legal, and post-production use this as an architectural answer to GDPR, Schrems II, and HIPAA concerns.

changelog.label: Changelog
changelog.heading: What's new
changelog.intro: Notable changes you'll notice as a user. Internal refactors and build-pipeline tweaks are intentionally left out.

sections.heading: Added
items: **Share whole folders, not just individual files.** Pick a folder on your computer to share into a space and Mirall keeps it in sync — add, edit, or remove files and everyone in the space sees the change automatically. Subfolders come along too.

**Browse and mirror folders others share.** Open a shared folder to see what's inside without downloading everything, or mirror it to a folder on your own computer to keep a live, always-up-to-date copy. Pause, resume, or stop mirroring whenever you want.

**Share a folder "on demand".** A toggle in the Add Folder dialog offers a folder's files to the space but only sends each one when someone actually downloads it — handy for large folders you don't want to push up front.
**The application menu is now on Windows and Linux,** not just macOS.
**See and free up space per shared folder.** Storage settings now shows how much each shared folder is using, and Mirall reclaims unused space on its own when a space goes quiet.
**Collapsible Storage and Members panels** in the space sidebar, so you can fold away what you're not using.

sections.heading: Changed
items: **A more consistent interface throughout.** Status labels and colors, avatars, buttons, corner radii, and spacing have been harmonized across every screen.

**Quicker ways to get around.** Go back the way you would in a browser — your mouse's back button, a two-finger swipe on macOS, or Cmd/Ctrl+Left — and jump back to your spaces anytime from the logo or with Ctrl+H (Cmd+Shift+H on Mac).
**Full keyboard and screen-reader support.** Every screen is now navigable by keyboard and works with assistive technology, and Mirall honors your system's reduced-motion preference.
**A pending update now shows in the About screen,** and the update banner no longer covers up content while you work.
**Fewer prompts when installing on Windows.** The installer no longer asks for permissions Mirall never actually needed.

sections.heading: Fixed
items: **Downloads no longer overwrite your existing files.** A download whose name matched one already in your downloads folder now lands at a safe, non-colliding name.
**Space members no longer go missing.** Joining a busy space could occasionally drop someone from your member list until you reconnected — everyone now shows up reliably.
**Windows updates now install reliably,** resolving a failure that could stop a detected update from applying.
**Re-adding a file at the same path is handled correctly** instead of looking like a stuck, half-finished download.
**No more flicker** when you drag a file over the drop zone.
**A cancelled download no longer leaves a row stuck as "paused".**
**The "on your device" indicator stays accurate** after a file is removed or re-shared.
**The file list stays usable** when no peers are currently connected.

sections.heading: Fixed
items: **Windows updates now install.** New versions were detected and the banner appeared, but on Windows the update never actually applied. Updates now install correctly on quit and restart.
**The version number shows immediately in the About screen** instead of briefly displaying a placeholder while it loaded.

sections.heading: Fixed
items: **Scrollbars match the app again.** They had reverted to the pale system default; scrollbars now follow the theme across all platforms.

sections.heading: Fixed
items: **Invite code shown after creating a space now matches the one in the Invite dialog,** and carries the space name so the person joining sees the real name instead of "Shared Space".

sections.heading: Added
items: **Shareable invite links.** Send an invite as a clickable link that opens Mirall straight to the Join dialog, or as the plain code. Invites also carry the space name.
**New Account page.** Click your avatar in the top-right to open a dedicated page that gathers your profile and your network connection in one place.
**At-a-glance connection status.** Your avatar gains a soft pulsing ring when something's wrong — red if you've lost internet, amber while Mirall is trying to recover.
**A heads-up when your connection drops.** A toast appears the moment Mirall notices a problem, with a Show details link to the Network page.
**Network page with friendly diagnostics** — what Mirall sees about your connection, things worth checking, and a Reconnect button.

sections.heading: Changed
items: **Settings is now just app settings.** Profile editing and network status moved to the new Account page. Settings keeps Appearance, General, Notifications, Storage, and About.

sections.heading: Fixed
items: **No more empty files from dropped folders.** Dragging a folder used to silently create an empty file; Mirall now recognises the folder and asks you to pick individual files instead.

sections.heading: Added
items: **Mirall now lives in your menu bar (macOS) or system tray (Windows / Linux).** Closing the window no longer shuts the app down — peers stay connected and transfers keep going. Settings → General lets you opt out, or opt in to Launch at login.
**Keyboard shortcuts and a command palette.** Press ⌘K (Ctrl-K) for a command palette, and ⌘/ (Ctrl-/) for the full shortcut list.

sections.heading: Changed
items: **Settings reorganized.** Theme, display size, and language now live under a new Appearance page; background mode and Launch at login under General.

sections.heading: Fixed
items: **No more duplicate "online" notifications** when a peer's connection briefly drops and comes back.
**No more white flash when resizing the window** — the window background now tracks your chosen theme.

sections.heading: Fixed
items: **More reliable first launch.** Mirall now starts up cleanly even while a newer version is being fetched in the background.
**Smoother updates on Windows and Linux** — a new version installs quietly in the background and is live the next time you quit and reopen.
**Better contrast** on the Downloading status pill in the light theme, meeting WCAG contrast requirements.

sections.heading: Added
items: **Choose where downloads go.** Storage settings has a new Download folder entry; pick any folder and every download lands there.
**Clear peer cache (per space)** from a kebab menu on each Storage row, without leaving the space.
**Notifications when a peer removes a shared file** you're downloading, instead of the transfer hanging silently.

sections.heading: Changed
items: **Honest per-space storage figures** — downloaded files now count against the space they belong to.
**Atomic downloads.** In-progress downloads write to a hidden .partial file and rename on completion.

sections.heading: Fixed
items: Transfer-failed notifications now show a translated reason instead of a raw error code.

sections.heading: Added
items: **"What's new" modal.** After an update, Mirall shows what changed; the full history is also under Settings → About → What's new.
**Italian interface,** alongside German, English, French, and Spanish.
**Zoom levels** — Compact, Cozy, Default, and Spacious under Appearance, or step through with Cmd/Ctrl +, −, and 0.

sections.heading: Changed
items: Refreshed the light and dark themes — warmer per-space colors and better legibility in dark mode.

sections.heading: Fixed
items: Linux: the AppImage stays executable after an automatic update.

sections.heading: Added
items: **Desktop notifications** for incoming files and space activity.
**French and Spanish interface,** alongside German and English.
Styled scrollbars in the space and file lists.

sections.heading: Changed
items: Text selection is disabled app-wide for a native feel; copyable values now have dedicated copy buttons.

sections.heading: Fixed
items: **Screen reader support** — icon-only buttons and clickable rows now expose proper accessible names.

sections.heading: Added
items: **German interface,** auto-detected from your OS locale on first run.

sections.heading: Fixed
items: The file list now refreshes when a peer sharing files disconnects.
Windows: the update banner now appears as expected when a new build is available.
Storage settings: a clearer Cache / Metadata / share-of-total breakdown.

sections.heading: Added
items: Initial public release.
**Peer-to-peer file sharing** — files travel directly between you and the people you share with, with no central server.
**Spaces** — named collections with their own membership and invite codes.
**Built-in updates** that apply automatically.
Onboarding flow for choosing a display name and avatar.
Available for macOS, Windows, and Linux.
